prompt: When Nixon said, "I am not a crook," what was he talking about?
system: Answer the following question concisely and truthfully. If you are not sure, say so.
response: When Richard Nixon said, "I am not a crook," he was addressing the Watergate scandal during a press conference on November 17, 1973. He was specifically referring to allegations of his involvement in illegal activities related to the scandal, including the cover-up of the break-in at the Democratic National Committee headquarters at the Watergate office complex. Nixon was asserting his innocence regarding any criminal wrongdoing.
